oracle_output_parsed.content: France to all: I propose we keep the peace in Western Europe for 1901. England, I will not move on you from Brest or Picardy this turn if you likewise do not attack my channels. Germany/Italy, I will not advance into Burgundy or Gascony this spring if you respect borders. I plan a safe opening: Brest to Gascony, Marseilles to Burgundy, Paris to Picardy — consolidating France and leaving openings for diplomacy. Reply if you want a non‑aggression understanding; silence will be taken as acceptance for Spring 1901 only.

England — quick note: I value a quiet English Channel opening. If you keep your fleets in the north (no CONVOY to France) I will keep Brest off of a direct attack on LON and instead play GAS/PIC. If you prefer to contest the Channel, tell me and we can discuss whether to cooperate against Germany instead. My expected move: F BRE-GAS, A PAR-PIC, A MAR-BUR. Please confirm your Spring intentions so we avoid immediate losses.

Germany — France is planning a modest, quiet opening (BRE->GAS, PAR->PIC, MAR->BUR). I'm open to a non‑aggression on Burgundy/Belgium so we don't clash early. If you agree to avoid an immediate push into BUR/BEL this spring, I will not support any English aggression into French coastal centers. If you disagree, please say so now.

Italy — heads up: Marseilles will likely move to Burgundy this spring. I have no intention of contesting the Tyrrhenian/Adriatic this turn. If you want an understanding over the Western Med (Naples/Rome vs Marseilles/Bur), propose it and I will consider supporting your moves against Austria or neutral coasts.
